title: cyathea chimborazensis
intro: Cyathea chimborazensis, also known as the Chimborazo tree fern, is a species of tree fern that belongs to the Cyathea genus and is native to Ecuador. The medicinal properties of Cyathea chimborazensis include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its leaves and rhizome. The health benefits of this plant include treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as wound healing and reducing inflammation. Herbal preparations of Cyathea chimborazensis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, and it is often used in traditional medicine in Ecuador.
